takeaways: Senior housing enters 2026 with clear momentum — demand is outpacing a historically thin development pipeline, and industry forecasts call for discounts to vanish as occupancy tightens.
CRE capital markets are reopening: the 10-Year Treasury has stabilized near 4.00–4.25%, markets expect at least one 25 bps cut, and Colliers projects a 15–20% jump in 2026 sales volume.
Agentic AI is moving from pilots to production in real estate — operators are redesigning workflows around AI agents for procurement, pricing, leasing, and portfolio decisioning.

items.headline: Senior living discounts set to vanish as demand outpaces thin pipeline
items.summary: PERE's 2026 Look Ahead reports senior living concessions will disappear in the US as demand from older adults accelerates against a historically thin development pipeline. For operators like Lloyd Jones, this signals pricing power returning and a window to push rate growth across independent and assisted living portfolios.

items.headline: 10-Year Treasury stabilizes near 4.00–4.25%, easing CRE financing
items.summary: Commercial loan rates now range 4.99–12.75% with conventional mortgages pricing 4.99–8.75%, anchored to a 10-Year Treasury that has held near 4.00–4.25% since mid-2025. Stabilizing benchmarks are restoring underwriting confidence for senior housing acquisitions and refinancings.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in 2026 CRE sales volume
items.summary: Capital is flowing again as institutional and cross-border buyers re-enter the market, with Colliers forecasting a 15–20% increase in 2026 sales volume. Markets anticipate at least one 25 bps rate cut early in the year, with inflation tracking near 3%, supporting a more dovish Fed stance.

items.headline: Agentic AI reshapes retail and real estate workflows in 2026
items.summary: ICSC reports agentic AI is the next phase of proptech, with operators deploying AI agents for procurement, pricing, inventory, leasing and event programming. For senior housing, this means automating resident onboarding, care coordination and dynamic pricing across portfolios.

items.headline: Microsoft: leaders scaling AI to drive enterprise transformation
items.summary: Microsoft reports that leaders are moving AI from experiments to enterprise-wide transformation, anchoring AI to business outcomes rather than tools. Firms that redesign end-to-end workflows are cutting cycle times materially — a template increasingly relevant to multi-site operators.

items.headline: Kisco Senior Living plans Summerlin community in Las Vegas
items.summary: Kisco Senior Living announced plans for a full-service community in Summerlin, Las Vegas offering independent living, assisted living and memory care. The project adds to a wave of Sun Belt senior housing development activity — a core Lloyd Jones growth market — even as overall starts remain constrained.

items.headline: 2026 senior living trends: demand leads, capital reopens
items.summary: Multi-Housing News reports senior housing is entering 2026 with strong demand tailwinds and reopening capital markets after a multi-year freeze. Median independent living runs $3,200/month, assisted living $5,419, and memory care $6,690 — with costs still rising and occupancy climbing.

items.headline: Senior living faces choose-your-own-adventure moment in 2026
items.summary: Senior Housing News frames 2026 as a pivotal year: operators who invest in workforce, technology and margin recovery now will separate from the pack as the demographic wave hits. Those who wait risk being squeezed by rising care acuity and labor costs.

items.headline: Interest rates spur 2026 debt capital growth for CRE
items.summary: CRE Daily reports that stabilizing inflation near 3% and a more flexible monetary policy stance are paving the way for stronger CRE debt liquidity in 2026. Improved lender participation and steadier borrowing costs are unlocking deal flow that stalled through 2023–2024.

items.headline: Deloitte: 2026 CRE outlook points to renewed momentum
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 commercial real estate outlook highlights improving fundamentals across most sectors, with capital access, leasing demand and pricing visibility all strengthening. The report flags senior housing and industrial as standout beneficiaries of the capital rotation.

items.headline: Proptech's new buzzword is 'workflow' as AI plumbs underlying data
items.summary: Commercial Observer reports agentic AI is stitching together data platforms and management tools to create the missing technology layer proptech has lacked. The result: end-to-end workflow automation is now the central investment theme for CRE operators in 2026.

items.headline: Real estate operators vet AI tools for measurable portfolio value
items.summary: ICSC reports owners are rigorously testing AI and proptech tools to prove measurable ROI across portfolios — with some experimenting with digital-twin models of cities to predict consumer spending and sales volumes. The vetting bar is rising as budgets tighten.

items.headline: Executive turnover slows sharply as firms retain AI-era talent
items.summary: Journal of Accountancy reports only 19% of executives saw >50% leadership turnover in the past 12 months, down from 43% a year ago. Companies are choosing to retain and develop top talent to navigate AI disruption rather than bring in outsiders.

items.headline: Strategic clarity remains top hindrance to leadership effectiveness
items.summary: DDI's 2026 Leadership Trends finds lack of clarity around strategic objectives and ineffective decision-making processes are the top two barriers to leadership effectiveness — for the second year running. Emotional intelligence is emerging as a core executive competency.

items.headline: 89% of workers link wellbeing to higher performance in Wellhub 2026 report
items.summary: Wellhub's State of Work-Life-Wellness 2026 finds 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, cementing wellness as a productivity lever. Employers adopting lifestyle medicine frameworks report measurable gains in talent retention and healthcare cost control.

items.headline: Monster rounds return to proptech as valuations re-expand
items.summary: PwC's Emerging Trends notes high valuations and large financings are reappearing in proptech, with investors paying premium prices for companies with proven distribution and category leadership. Capital is concentrating in AI-native operating platforms.

items.headline: Aspen Institute on what great business leadership looks like in 2026
items.summary: The Aspen Institute argues great 2026 leaders pair AI fluency with human-centered judgment, ethics and empathy. With leaner teams post-restructuring, human workload has not disappeared — making emotional intelligence a differentiator rather than a nice-to-have.

items.headline: Global Wellness Institute: workplace wellbeing trends for 2026
items.summary: The Global Wellness Institute highlights a shift toward evidence-based, personalized wellbeing programs in the workplace. Expect biomarker-driven nutrition, AI wellness coaches, and mental-health-first benefit design to dominate 2026 HR budgets.

items.headline: 'Movement snacks' and nervous-system resets top 2026 wellness trends
items.summary: Dr. Axe and Global Wellness Summit flag short 'movement snacks' throughout the day as the new alternative to long workouts, while about 45% of adults report burnout — driving demand for nervous system regulation practices. Regulation is replacing hustle as a workplace norm.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as a new health category for 2026
items.summary: Who What Wear reports 'emotional fitness' is becoming a recognized health category alongside physical and cognitive wellbeing, with emphasis on resilience, regulation and personalized data. The trend has implications for both workforce wellness programs and senior living resident services.
